title: Laelia cochlearioides
taxonomy: Laelia cochlearioides is a species of orchid, belonging to the genus Laelia. It is classified under the family Orchidaceae and is native to Central America. The specific epithet cochlearioides refers to the shape of its flowers, which resemble those of the Cochlearia plant. Laelia cochlearioides is a terrestrial orchid that grows in tropical forests and is known for its beautiful, long-lasting flowers. The classification of Laelia cochlearioides is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Liliopsida, and order Asparagales.
